---
# Source Card: Age- and sex-dependent clinical equations to estimate glomerular filtration rates in children and young adults with chronic kidney disease

## Summary
This peer-reviewed CKiD study (Pierce et al., Kidney International 2021; DOI 10.1016/j.kint.2020.10.047; PMID 33301749) derives the "CKiD under 25 (U25)" eGFR equations for children and young adults aged 1-25 years with CKD, in two biomarker families (height/serum-creatinine and 1/cystatin-C) each with a sex-dependent and a sex-and-age-dependent multiplier K. For the pediatric CDS module it supplies exact, assay-standardized K constants (dimensionless multipliers yielding mL/min/1.73 m2), the age/sex banding logic, and the analyzer/calibration dependence those constants assume.

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) Equation forms eGFR = K x (height/sCr) and eGFR = K x (1/cysC), mL/min/1.73 m2, two K variants each — locator: Abstract/Methods — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:c26cf4caea1367dbe70c3bf4d6feb6553a993d7d2dc0ab204fcc658a7db908a1]"
- (ev_002) Sex-specific constant sCr K = 41.8 (M) / 37.6 (F) — locator: Abstract/Results — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:e23ff8c094b5cb1e040ddfe1b0a1abc92701c92bbb882efcfd261f4da768bfd7]"
- (ev_003) Age+sex-dependent sCr K: males 35.7 (age 1) to 50.8 (>=18); females 33.1 to 41.4 — locator: Abstract/Results — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:b3b9a01c13a4f16d31f2e6ff49f9b1f61f7c300630edd0d186270a5790d4b958]"
- (ev_004) Constant cysC K = 81.9 (M) / 74.9 (F), cysC in mg/L — locator: Results — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:d8f84d4b66e74e9ace4fbcef1acf9f84e23b3baf92c8ac0212201f0343ec55db]"
- (ev_005) Age+sex-dependent cysC K peaks: age 15 M (K=87.2), age 12 F (K=79.9) — locator: Abstract/Results — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:5766355af6be07744570bb0309ae5f25b44dc40a01df2492d72367ef97f2174c]"
- (ev_006) Assay dependence: sCr enzymatic (Advia 2400, Siemens, IDMS); cysC nephelometric (Siemens, IFCC) — locator: Methods — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:5347a546edc4c42fb57ddb59a5ecf77615797166020a9ec70652c4bd25b72270]"
- (ev_007) Only the two U25 age-dependent equations reached non-significant bias AND P30 >85% in both children and young adults; no race coefficient — locator: Abstract/Discussion — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:715712f542a50586152e534f853ec5f486bf3c6a12d772f31040aa75992ec66c]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population: children and young adults aged 1-25 years with mild-to-moderate CKD (CKiD cohort, 63 US/Canada sites; median mGFR ~48 mL/min/1.73 m2); both sexes; reference standard is plasma iohexol-disappearance mGFR.
- Assay/method dependence: K constants assume enzymatic IDMS-standardized serum creatinine (mg/dL) and IFCC-standardized nephelometric cystatin C (mg/L); height must be in meters. Constants are not transferable to non-standardized assays.
- Age banding: age-dependent K uses piecewise-linear knots (sCr: 12 and 18 y; cysC: 15/18 y males, 12/18 y females) with K held constant for ages 18-25. The published age-specific K table is given for integer ages and is intended to be applied within +/-0.5 y of the listed integer age (e.g., ages 12.5-13.5 use the age-13 K).
- Lifecycle: effective 2021-04; supersedes the CKiD 2009 bedside sCr equation (single K=41.3, age/sex-independent) and the CKiD 2012 cystatin-C equation. No author-declared retirement date.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- The source explicitly bounds its own scope: it does not validate the equations in children/young adults free of CKD and cannot speak to screening otherwise-healthy patients ("we cannot speak to the validity of our equations for estimating GFR in healthy patients or to their clinical utility for screening otherwise healthy patients"). A CDS use outside 1-25 y CKD, or on unstandardized assays, is a scope boundary, not a supported use.

## Limitations & conflicts
- Internal validation only (CKiD subset); the authors performed no external validation of the U25 equations — performance claims are for the internal CKiD validation set.
- sCr-based equations require patient height, which "may not always be available to laboratories."
- GAP (not asserted): the full integer-age K lookup table (per-year K values ages 1-18) is in a supplementary table not reproduced in the retrieved full text; only the endpoint/peak values (ev_002-ev_005) carry verbatim numbers here. Per-year interior K values are NOT locatable in the retrieved text and must not be inferred — record as "per-age U25 K table not locatable in retrieved PMC full text."
- GAP (not asserted): exact piecewise per-year growth factors appear in the body with mangled formatting (e.g., "1.008 per year") but the underlying anchor constants (39.0, 36.1) render unreliably in the retrieved text; treated as non-verbatim and therefore not asserted as thresholds.
- No direct conflict recorded with the other pediatric-CDS sources; this source intentionally supersedes (not contradicts averaged) the earlier CKiD 2009 bedside (K=41.3) and CKiD 2012 cysC constants — the supersession is preserved in lifecycle fields, not collapsed.
